items.headline: MiSTer Companion v3.8.0 Released
items.message:
MiSTer Companion v3.8.0 is now available.

This release adds RetroAchievement Cores to the Extras tab. They are installed separately from your regular cores and use their own MiSTer_RA.ini, so your existing MiSTer setup remains untouched.

The included MiSTer main binary is installed as MiSTer_RA, so it does not overwrite your stable MiSTer main. During installation, MiSTer Companion duplicates your current MiSTer.ini as MiSTer_RA.ini and adds the RetroAchievement core entries at the bottom, keeping your existing video, CRT, controller, and other custom settings intact.

A new Edit Config option is also included, allowing you to configure your RetroAchievements username, password, popup settings, leaderboards, debug logging, and hardcore mode directly from MiSTer Companion.

To use the RetroAchievement-enabled cores, configure RetroAchievements first, then select MiSTer_RA.ini from the MiSTer OSD menu and launch the cores from the _RA Cores folder.

The Extras tab now also includes a manual Check for Updates button for each entry. Automatic update checks could quickly hit GitHub's API rate limit, so manual checks make the Extras tab more reliable and avoid unnecessary GitHub requests.

You can download the latest stable release below.

items.headline: Help Bring MiSTer Companion to macOS
items.message:
macOS support is now being actively worked on, with recent improvements contributed by DoggyChicken.

To continue improving and properly maintain macOS support long-term, having access to a Mac for development and testing is still essential. At the moment, I rely on community contributions and am unable to test macOS-specific changes directly.

If you'd like to help make this possible, consider supporting the Ko-fi goal below.
